compositional_truth_check.details: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly identifiable as distinct specimens; the lower pot appears to contain a singular, struggling sprout rather than the expected multi-plant arrangement.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_string_of_nickels: Present in yellow pot, showing signs of dehydration.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_mexican_mint: Systemic Loss/Unidentifiable. No evidence of mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_pothos: Present with white rabbit anchor; significant leaf chlorosis/necrosis.

plant_audit.p1: Yellow pot: Foliage is sparse and dull, indicating low vigor.
plant_audit.p3: Black pot: Primary leaf is heavily necrotic (brown/yellowing). The rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.lower_pot: Contains a single, isolated green sprout. Lacks the density of a healthy P2/P4 biome.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Surface appears dry and compacted with some white mineral/perlite deposits.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_5_to_current: Progressive decline in P3 leaf health (increasing brown area). The sprout in the lower pot has remained largely static in posture, suggesting minimal growth or potential stasis.

visual_health_inference.p3: Critical. The large leaf is undergoing senescence or severe nutrient/water stress.
visual_health_inference.lower_sprout: Stable but isolated; health is indeterminate due to lack of comparative growth.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of physiological stress. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting advanced tissue death on its primary leaf. The String of Nickels (P1) shows signs of neglect. The lower pot, intended for P2/P4, is effectively barren save for a single, solitary sprout, suggesting a failure of the intended planting or a significant die-off event prior to the observation window.
